rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD LITKE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Granting Reformation Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/05/2023 (no earlier date available): 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) 26 CFR 26.2654-1(b)(1)(ii)(B) requires a reformation proceeding, if required, to be commenced prior to the filing deadline for the estate tax return, but this petition does not appear to have been filed prior to the deadline. Has the IRS granted an extension for the judicial reformation sought by the petition? 4) Was either drafting attorney licensed to practice law in California when the subject trust governed by California law was prepared for the settlor as a California resident client? 5) How did the status of the settlor's health, his location in San Francisco, and his concerns about the imminent death cause the omission of the requirement intended by the settlor to divide the Residual Trust into GST tax exempt and nonexempt trusts in the event the settlor was to predecease his spouse? Is either drafting attorney admitting that he caused the error? Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAMBERT GENZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural Issues on both petitions: (1) It only lists that the decedent was survived by two of other next of kin. Pursuant to LRSF 14.18.C.3), Petition needs to provide detail ancestors' info so that the court can review the notice requirement under PC 8110(b). (2) Proposed Order to be submitted. Additional Procedural Issues on Ms. Qubao Yu's Petition: (1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] to be served to Public Administrator and (2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License to be submitted. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Michelle D Urias Verified Petition For Approval Of The Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the settlement agreement purportedly signed by Michelle D. Uria, Brenda Schenthal, as agent under Miguel Uria Sr.'s DPA, and Miguel Uria, and the attorneys for Michelle D. Uria and for Brenda Schenthal should be approved. 1) The settlement agreement was not actually signed by any of the parties or their attorneys; all of the signatures appear to be electronic, but there is no indication that they were electronically signed by each party or attorney. 2) The proof of service filed on 1/19/23 was the only one that attached the necessary mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing (DE-120), on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (If a pleading form of proof of service is prepared, a copy of the actual Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached. It is the Notice of Hearing that shows all the pertinent information an interested party is entitled to know: the case name and number, identity of the petitioner, the hearing date, time, and place, and the full title of the petition to be heard.) This Notice of Hearing, however, was mailed only to the attorney for Brenda Schenthal and to Elena Maria Uria. Notice must be given directly to an interested party and also to an attorney who represents an interested party, but not only to the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1214 and CRC 7.51(a) and (b).] PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AKIKO SPINDLE 2021 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amended pleading was filed on 1/23/23 (but not e-filed until 1/26/23, when it became available for viewing on the register of actions), which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. The notice of hearing showed the wrong hearing date and also did not clearly identify the pleading in item 1, which should have indicated that a "FIRST AMENDED FIRST ACCOUNT AND REPORT OF TRUSTEE," etc., had been filed. Furthermore, mailing notice on 1/30/23 for a hearing on 2/22/23 would not have been sufficient notice for a trust petition. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] It is unclear from the records (in the related conservatorship case as well) WHEN assets were transferred to the trustee. No receipt has been filed by the conservator. No informational I&A was filed by the trustee. The conservator's third accounting ended on 11/30/21 but did not reflect any distributions to fund this trust..? The only statements filed so far are the ones for the ML accounts xx8825 and xx8826 for the periods 10/30/21 - 12/31/21 and 6/1/22 - 6/30/22. Statements verifying the BEGINNING balances have not been filed. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2) and CRC 7.903(c).] (Statements filed on 9/6/22 were not "lost'; they were apparently filed conventionally.) The "fiduciary acquisition values" have not been able to be verified. Some of the market values shown on Schedule F do not agree with the statements. See, for example, US Treasury Inflation Note .375% due 7/15/2027, US Treasury Inflation Note 0.125% due 4/15/2025, Los Angeles Calif Wtr & Power Rev 5.00% due 7/1/2027, Verizon Communications 4.125% due 3/16/2027, and others. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/7/23. If the accounting is amended again and there is insufficient time to give 30 days' notice of the amended accounting petition after it is e-filed, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request an additional continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Terminate Trust, To Determine Ownership Of Property, And For Transfer Of The Property To The Probate Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/18/23. (No earlier available date.) Any person, other than a trustee or beneficiary, whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition (e.g., an heir) must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(a)(2) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] The initial petition and other petitions filed in this proceeding never disclosed that the decedent's predeceased brorhter was survived by issue. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL LINDA BACHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] How could there be gains on sale, when no assets were sold? The prayer states a different amount of fees than the fees shown in paragraph 18. A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/10/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BERNARD J. ROBERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The original will was only just lodged on 2/6/23 and has not yet been provided to the examiner to review. Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1 D.6. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Item 7b(3) of the petition is checked, but the wife died less than five years before the decedent. Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust (here, the Survivor's Trust under the Robert Family Trust). If the trustee is the petitioner, all of the trust beneficiaries must be listed on Attachment 8 of the petition and clearly identified as such. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Are the siblings listed in item 8 of the petition all of their issue (other than Decedent)? The brother survived the decedent. His successor-in-interest must be clearly identified. Has an executor or administrator been appointed in his estate or did Leendert Guustaaf Robert have a trust? The testator named Family Radio in Oakland, California, as a beneficiary. Is the one listed in item 8 of the petition, located in Franklin, TN, the same organization? Item 9 of the petition is incomplete. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/30/23 to avoid a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEATRIZ QUITOLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. If the value of the real property is $345,000 and encumbrance is $44,000, the net value of the estate should be $301,000. Notwithstanding Probate Code Section 8468, LRSF 14.24 requires a written declination to be filed by or on behalf of an individual who has priority for appointment but does not desire to act, and if no declination is filed, the petition must indicate the reason. Although the son cannot be appointed, as he is not a resident of the US [Probate Code Section 8402(a)(4).], he could still have nominated someone in the US to serve [Probate Code Section 8465(a)(2).]. It is unclear if he is/was aware of his right to nominate someone or if he declined. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. (It is not necessary for an attorney to file a declaration to state that papers will be filed or submitted. Papers should simply be filed or submitted.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY ANNE MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The original will file was just created on 2/6/23 and has not yet been provided to the examiner. Item 6f of the petition is not the first box that applies if the decedent was survived by siblings. Were the two predeceased brothers, Lowell Lee Martin and Thomas Lee Martin, not survived by issue? "Beneficiary" is not a relationship: one is either a family member or a "stranger". What are the ages of the minor beneficiaries, Aron Domingues and Eduardo Domingues? Notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] The will did not include most last names. How did the petitioner determine who the intended beneficiaries are? A supplement to the petition must be filed, signed and verified by the petitioner, to address these issues. Courtesy copies of the supplement and proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/7/23 to avoid a continuance. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
